# onnx2oracle

> Load ONNX embedding models into Oracle AI Database with one command.

onnx2oracle is a Python CLI and library that downloads sentence-transformer models from HuggingFace, augments them with an ONNX tokenizer, pooling, and L2 normalization, and registers them in Oracle AI Database 23ai or 26ai. Once loaded, `VECTOR_EMBEDDING(MODEL USING :text AS DATA)` runs entirely in-database. No sidecar service, no network hop.

## Install

Path A, local Docker:

```
pip install onnx2oracle
onnx2oracle docker up --wait
onnx2oracle load all-MiniLM-L6-v2 --target local
onnx2oracle verify --target local
```

Path B, existing Oracle 23ai or 26ai instance:

```
pip install onnx2oracle
export ORACLE_DSN='user/password@host:port/service'
onnx2oracle load all-MiniLM-L6-v2
onnx2oracle verify
```

Required privileges: `CREATE MINING MODEL`, `EXECUTE ON DBMS_VECTOR`, `SELECT ANY DICTIONARY`.

## Presets

- all-MiniLM-L6-v2: 384 dims, ~90 MB, mean pool. Default. Fastest, smallest.
- all-MiniLM-L12-v2: 384 dims, ~130 MB, mean pool. Slightly better quality than L6.
- all-mpnet-base-v2: 768 dims, ~420 MB, mean pool. Best quality among mean-pooled English models.
- bge-small-en-v1.5: 384 dims, ~130 MB, CLS pool. Strong retrieval benchmarks.
- nomic-embed-text-v1: 768 dims, ~540 MB, mean pool. 8192-token context for long documents.

Custom HuggingFace models via `onnx2oracle load NAME --from-huggingface REPO`. SentencePiece-based tokenizers (e5, XLM-R) are not supported and raise `NotImplementedError`; use WordPiece models.

## Query

```
SELECT VECTOR_EMBEDDING(ALL_MINILM_L6_V2 USING 'hello world' AS DATA) AS v
FROM dual;
```

Wire it into a table:

```
CREATE TABLE docs (
  id        NUMBER PRIMARY KEY,
  content   CLOB,
  embedding VECTOR(384, FLOAT32)
);

INSERT INTO docs (id, content, embedding)
VALUES (1, :text, VECTOR_EMBEDDING(ALL_MINILM_L6_V2 USING :text AS DATA));
```

Dimension must match the preset: 384 for MiniLM and BGE-small, 768 for MPNet and Nomic.

- [Model matrix](https://jasperan.github.io/onnx2oracle/reference/model-matrix.html)
- [CLI reference](https://jasperan.github.io/onnx2oracle/reference/cli.html)
- [Troubleshooting](https://jasperan.github.io/onnx2oracle/reference/troubleshooting.html)

- [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/onnx2oracle/)

## Troubleshooting

- ORA-00942: user_mining_models does not exist. Oracle 23ai or 26ai is required, and the user needs dictionary access.
- ORA-20000: LOAD_ONNX_MODEL failed. Rebuild with onnx>=1.16, opset 18. The metadata input tensor must be named `pre_text`.
- ORA-01031: insufficient privileges. Grant EXECUTE ON DBMS_VECTOR and CREATE MINING MODEL to the DB user.
- NotImplementedError about SentencePiece. Use a WordPiece-based model instead (e.g. microsoft/Multilingual-MiniLM-L12-H384).
- onnx2oracle docker up hangs on first run. Normal. PDB cold start takes 3 to 5 minutes. Tail `onnx2oracle docker logs -f` to confirm progress.
- Docker permission denied. Add the user to the `docker` group, or use an existing Oracle instance via ORACLE_DSN.

## Agent rules

- Never commit credentials. ORACLE_DSN belongs in the environment.
- Always run `verify` after `load`. Report the four-line output verbatim.
- If the user says "default" and doesn't name a model, pick all-MiniLM-L6-v2.
- Pass `--force` only after confirming with the user if a model name already exists.
- Oracle identifiers are uppercase with underscores. The CLI normalizes HF-style names automatically.
